A WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE INDUSTRIPLEX AREA AND MOVED EAST SOUTHEAST ON A INTERMITTENT PATH TO JUST NORTHEAST EAST OF INTERSECTION OF STUMBERG LANE AND AIRLINE HIGHWAY. SURVEY DETERMINED A TRACK STARTING IN ST HELENA PARISH AT THE INTERSECTION OF LA 441 AND LA 1048, PROCEEDING EAST ALONG LA 1048 INTO TANGIPAHOA PARISH, LIFTING NEAR U.S. HIGHWAY 51 AT ARCOLA. LARGE PINES AND OAKS UPROOTED. MOST TREES DOWN NOTED ALONG ARCOLA ROAD AT WIDEST POINT NEAR 120 YARDS WIDE. TRACK LENGTH 6.14 MILES. SOME SHINGLES REMOVED OFF ONE HOME. SMALL CANOPIES AND TRAMPOLINES MANGLED. ESTIMATED 85 MPH.

A NARROW BUT LINEAR TRACK WAS DETECTED AT I-55/ARCOLA EXIT, THEN ON SIDE ROADS OFF HIGHWAY 1048, MINOR TREE DAMAGE TO SMALL DIAMETER PINE TREES, LIFTING NEAR RUSSELL TOWN ROAD. LENGTH 3.80 MILES, WIDTH 25 YARDS, ESTIMATED 75 MPH WINDS. DISTINCTLY APART FROM OTHER TORNADO IN CLOSE PROXIMITY ABOUT 1/2 MILE NORTH OF THIS TRACK.

TORNADO SIGHTED BY PUBLIC TOUCHING DOWN VERY CLOSE TO THE CARRIERE QUICK STOP ON U.S. HIGHWAY 11, REMOVING A PIECE OF TIN FROM THE AWNING AND STRIKING A VEHICLE. TORNADO CROSSED HIGHWAY AND STRUCK THE FIRST SOUTHERN BANK BRANCH OFFICE, REMOVING THE ENTIRE AWNING ACROSS THE FRONT DECK AND LIGHTING. MATERIAL HURLED INTO THE YARDS OF RESIDENCES ALONG OLD HIGHWAY 11 AND SUSPENDED IN TREES. LARGE OAK TREE BRANCHES BROKEN IN FAMILY DOLLAR PARKING LOT. ESTIMATED 75 MPH. LENGTH 0.2 MILES, WIDTH 25 YDS.

PORTIONS OF THE STEVENS MEAT PROCESSING PLANT ROOF WAS REMOVED ON 52 FOOTS STEVENS ROAD NEAR SILVER RUN. A BARN NEARBY ALSO SUSTAINED DAMAGE. COMBINATION OF YOUTUBE STORM CHASER VIDEO AND STORM SURVEY INDICATED A MULTIPLE VORTEX WATERSPOUT MOVED THROUGH THE LONG BEACH HARBOR, REMOVING THE ROOF OFF AN OUTBUILDING AND TOPPLING TWO TALL MASTED SAIL BOATS. WATERSPOUT PROCEEDED ONSHORE ONTO THE BEACH AND CROSSED U.S. HIGHWAY 90 NEAR NICHOLSON STREET, LIFTING IN THE REAR OF ST. THOMAS THE APOSTLE CATHOLIC CHURCH. MEASURED TRACK LENGTH FROM VIDEO FOOTAGE IS 0.57 MILES, WIDTH 75 YARDS. ESTIMATED 70 MPH WINDS.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE COMMUNITY OF ST MARTIN JUST SOUTH OF LEMOYNE BLVD AND TRACKED NORTHEAST ON AN INTERMITTENT PATH ENDING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF INTERSTATE 10 AND MS HIGHWAY 609. THE DAMAGE RANGED FROM SHINGLE AND SKIRT REMOVAL TO A SMALL WOODEND SHED BEING DESTROYED. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH WAS 0.6 MILES, WIDTH OF 30 YARDS AND PRODUCED WINDS UP TO 75 MPH EF-0.

MULTIPLE SINGLE ENGINE AIRCRAFT DAMAGED AT BTR HANGERS. ONE AIRCRAFT WAS PICKED UP AND THROWN 1000 FEET. ITS A TOTAL LOSS. THIS WAS REPORTED FROM BTR AIRPORT OPERATIONS MANAGER.

*** 2 INJ *** A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 60, MOVED NNE ACROSS HIGHWAY 439 AND CONTINUED TO JUST NORTH OF MACK ADAMS OBERRY ROAD. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. A SINGLE FAMILY HOME HAD A LARGE PORTION OF ITS ROOF REMOVED AND ONE EXTERIOR WALL BLOWN IN. SEVERAL TRAILERS WERE HEAVILY DAMAGED INCLUDING ONE ROLLED TRAILER, INJURING ITS TWO OCCUPANTS. TOTAL DAMAGE PATH APPROXIMATELY 2.25 MILES, MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED AROUND 200 YARDS WIDE, MAXIMUM WIND SPEED 115MPH, RATED EF-2.

TORNADO BEGAN ALONG LA HWY 1049 AND CONTINUED EAST NORTHEAST TO NEAR LA HWY 1051. STRONGEST DAMAGE OCCURRED ALONG WREN ST. MULTIPLE TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. NUMEROUS LARGE BRACHES DOWN. ONE MOBILE HOME DESTROYED FROM UPROOTED LARGE PINE TREE. OTHER MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMGAGE OCCURRED DUE TO FALLING TREES AND LOSS OF SOME SHINGLES. TOTAL DAMAGE PATH APPROXIMATELY 0.6 MILES, MAX ESTIMATED WIDTH AROUND 50 YARDS, MAX WIND SPEED ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH. TORNADO RATED EF1.
ROOF COLLASPED ON HOUSE ON LA 439 NEAR NASH HARPER ROAD.

A TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN NEAR MILITARY ROAD, NORTH OF BROOKEDALE ROAD, AND CONTINUED NNE TO JUST NORTH OF HH WILLIAMS ROAD. LARGE NUMBER OF TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. AN OUTBUILDING WAS DESTORYED. SEVERAL CAMPER TRAILERS ROLLED. A SINGLE FAMILY HOME HAD PART OF THE ROOF REMOVED WITH DAMAGE TO THE PORCH. PATH LENGTH 0.75 MILES, ESTIMATED WIDTH 200 YARDS, MAXIMUM WINDS 115MPH, RATED EF-2.
TREES DOWN ON MULTIPLE ROADS.

TORNADO BEGAN JUST NORTH OF MEDICAL CENTER RD AND CONTINUED EAST CROSSING VETERANS AVE AND LIFTED AT THE EDGE OF THE WOODS. MULIPLE HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES SNAPPED AND NUMEROUS LARGE BRANCHES BROKEN. MINOR TO MODERATE STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A FEW SMALL PROFESSIONAL BUILDINGS AND A HOTEL. ONE BUILDING HAD MULTIPLE DORMERS DAMAGED OR RIPPED OFF OF THE ROOF. PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 0.5 MILE, WIDTH AROUND 75 YARDS, MAX ESTIMATED WINDS 100 MPH. TORNADO RATED EF1.

TOUCHED DOWN ON GOVERNEMNT STREET. TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST DOWNING 2 POWER POLES AND 3 SMALL TRAFFIC WARNING LIGHTS ON HWY 90. IT ALSO BROKE 7 WINDOWS AND DAMAGED CARS AT WAL-MART. IT CONTINUED NORTHEAST CAUSING DAMAGE TO TREES AND MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAMGE MAINLY TO SHINGLES AND FASCIA. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST NORTHEAST OF TAPP ROAD. TOTAL DAMAGE PATH APPROXIMATELY 3.5 MILES. MAX WIDTH EST 100 YDS. MAX WINDS EST 105 MPH. RATED EF-1.

TOUCHED DOWN OVER PASCAGOULA RIVER AND CAUSED DAMAGE AS IT MOVED ACROSS RIVERLODGE DR WHERE IT TORE THE PORCH ROOF OFF OF ONE HOME AND ALSO CAUSED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE ON TWO ADJACENT HOMES. AS IT MOVED NORTHEAST IT SNAPPED NUMEROUS HARD AND SOFT WOOD TREES AND CAUSED MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A FEW HOMES AS WELL AS THE FOUR MILE CREEK BAPTIST CHURCH. IT LIFTED AS IT CROSSED BLACK CREEK. TOTAL DAMAGE PATH APPROXIMATELY 4.1 MILES. MAX WIDTH EST 100 YDS. MAX WINDS EST 105 MPH. RATED EF-1.
